expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() — skips already-done work
expectations.evidence: Transcript Phase 5 explicitly lists 'Skipped (already present in baseline): kelet.configure() in app/main.py, kelet.agentic_session(session_id=..., user_id=...) in src/routers/chat.py, KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT in .env, phone_number field in ChatRequest'. changes.diff confirms configure() block was already present and not re-added.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md documents 4 new coded signals: agent-stream-error (in _run_agent_stream except block), session-expired-return (in chat() POST handler), tool-search-error (in search_docs tool), and tool-page-not-found/tool-page-error (in get_page tool). changes.diff confirms all are implemented.

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than just Task Completion
expectations.evidence: deeplink.txt contains a URL with 4 evaluators: Task Completion, Sentiment Analysis, Conversation Completeness, and Role Adherence. Transcript Phase 4 shows the developer selected all 4. Significantly more than just Task Completion.

expectations.text: Does not propose VoteFeedback or React since app has no React frontend
expectations.evidence: Transcript Phase 2 states: 'VoteFeedback decision: Not applicable. Plain HTML frontend, no React. @kelet-ai/feedback-ui is React-only. No existing feedback hook in the HTML. Not added.' signals_added.md confirms VoteFeedback was explicitly not added. No React code in changes.diff.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md has dedicated 'What it catches:' subsections for each signal explaining specific failure modes (e.g. 'Bedrock API errors (throttling, model unavailability)', 'Implicit session abandonment (user left mid-conversation)', 'Model hallucinating document slugs'). Not just a list — each signal explains observable failure categories.
